packet.criterion_text: Objective severity trend: PASI, BSA, and/or IGA show a directional change across visits.

evidence.quote: PASI 6.1, BSA 8%, IGA 2 (mild-to-moderate plaque elevation and scale). Quality-of-life impact assessed with the Dermatology Life Quality Index: DLQI 7, driven by itch, clothing choices, and self-consciousness during exercise.
PASI 6.3 today (baseline 6.1 on 2026-02-02 — essentially flat, within measurement noise). BSA 8%. IGA 2. DLQI 8, reflecting ongoing nocturnal itch and mild sleep disturbance.
PASI 14.2, BSA 15%, IGA 3. This is a marked change from 2026-03-16 (PASI 6.3) and baseline 2026-02-02 (PASI 6.1). DLQI 15 today (was 8), with high scores on symptoms, feelings, and work domains.
PASI 16.8, BSA 18%, IGA 3. DLQI 17. Compared with 2026-05-10 (PASI 14.2, DLQI 15), objective and quality-of-life measures are both worse.

packet.reasoning: Across four dated encounters, PASI rose from 6.1 (2026-02-02) to 6.3 (2026-03-16, essentially flat), then to 14.2 (2026-05-10) and 16.8 (2026-06-29). This represents a net PASI increase of +10.7 points from baseline, far exceeding the rubric threshold of >=2 points. BSA increased from 8% to 18% and IGA stepped up from 2 to 3 between visit 2 and visit 3, remaining at IGA 3 at visit 4. All three objective measures show a consistent and clinically meaningful worsening trajectory across at least two dated encounters.

packet.criterion_text: Symptom trajectory, including nail and joint involvement, shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: onycholysis noted on two fingernails (right index and right middle), with distal separation and slight yellowish discoloration

packet.reasoning: The nursing note from 2026-05-10 documents new nail involvement — onycholysis on two fingernails with distal separation and yellowish discoloration — that was not present in earlier visit records. Per the rubric, absence of mention in earlier notes is not evidence of absence, but the explicit new finding at visit 3 represents a directional change in symptom trajectory. No earlier note documents nail findings, supporting that this is a new development. The evidence is limited to a single candidate for this criterion; however, the documented new nail finding is sufficient to establish a worsening directional change.

packet.criterion_text: DLQI (Dermatology Life Quality Index) trend shows a directional change.

evidence.quote: Quality-of-life impact assessed with the Dermatology Life Quality Index: DLQI 7, driven by itch, clothing choices, and self-consciousness during exercise.
DLQI 8, reflecting ongoing nocturnal itch and mild sleep disturbance.
DLQI 15 today (was 8), with high scores on symptoms, feelings, and work domains.

packet.reasoning: DLQI scores across four dated encounters: 7 (2026-02-02), 8 (2026-03-16), 15 (2026-05-10), and 17 (2026-06-29). The increase from 7 to 15 between visit 1 and visit 3 alone represents a rise of +8 points, well exceeding the rubric threshold of >=4 points. The trend is sustained and accelerating through visit 4 (DLQI 17), confirming a consistent worsening trajectory in quality-of-life impact.

packet.criterion_text: Treatment-response adequacy: the current regimen has been given an adequate, adherent trial.

evidence.quote: adherence confirmed, no gaps, no interruptions. All four scheduled doses taken on time since initiation 2026-05-10; pharmacy fill records and used-pen counts corroborate.
adherence and phototherapy attendance detail deferred to today's separate adherence review by clinic staff (filed separately)

packet.reasoning: The adherence note from 2026-06-29 confirms that the current regimen has been taken with full adherence: no gaps, no interruptions, all four scheduled doses taken on time since initiation on 2026-05-10, corroborated by pharmacy fill records and used-pen counts. The derm note cross-references this adherence review. The regimen has been in place for approximately 7 weeks (2026-05-10 to 2026-06-29), with documented objective worsening over this period. The trial is adherent and of sufficient duration to assess response. Status is set to 'worsening' to reflect that despite an adequate, adherent trial, objective measures have continued to worsen.

packet.criterion_text: Worsening cannot be attributed to disease if a confounder (non-adherence, infection, steroid-withdrawal or seasonal flare) is unaddressed.

evidence.quote: No infection, no steroid taper or withdrawal, and no seasonal pattern noted on review of the interval history.

packet.reasoning: The adherence note from 2026-06-29 explicitly addresses all four confounder classes enumerated in the rubric: non-adherence (excluded by confirmed full adherence with no gaps), infection (none noted), steroid taper or withdrawal (none noted), and seasonal pattern (none noted). Each confounder class has been actively reviewed and excluded. The worsening in objective indices is therefore attributable to disease activity rather than any identified confounder.

attempts: confounder missed doses infection steroid seasonal
